differences.title: 1. Routing
differences.leftDesc: Nested routes are core, simplifying complex layouts and data fetching.
differences.rightDesc: App and Pages routers offer flexibility, with dynamic routing capabilities.

differences.title: 2. Data Loading
differences.leftDesc: Functions defined alongside routes fetch data before rendering.
differences.rightDesc: Specific functions handle server-side or static data fetching.

differences.title: 3. Mutations
differences.leftDesc: Handles POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE requests, forms are first-class.
differences.rightDesc: Requires explicit API routes or new Server Actions.

differences.title: 4. Server Rendering
differences.leftDesc: Primarily focuses on server-side rendering for dynamic content.
differences.rightDesc: Offers multiple rendering strategies for optimal performance.

differences.title: 5. Error Handling
differences.leftDesc: Errors are handled at the route level, simplifying UI.
differences.rightDesc: Utilizes React Error Boundaries for component-level error management.

differences.title: 6. SEO
differences.leftDesc: Strong SEO capabilities with meta tags and server rendering.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent SEO with static generation and dynamic rendering options.

differences.title: 7. Philosophy
differences.leftDesc: Embraces web fundamentals like HTTP, Request/Response.
differences.rightDesc: Provides a higher-level abstraction over React and Node.js.
